title: Europe celebrates 2026 heritage award winners in Cyprus
content:
On 28 May, some 500 guests and many more viewers online from the heritage community joined the 2026 European Heritage Awards Ceremony in Nicosia (Cyprus) to celebrate the 30 winners of the European Heritage Awards/ Europa Nostra Awards.

During the ceremony, which formed the highlight of the Cultural Heritage Summit 2026, the laureates of the five Grand Prix and the Public Choice Award selected from this year’s winners were revealed.

Grand Prix and Public Choice Award winners of the European Heritage Awards/ Europa Nostra Awards announced during European Heritage Summit in Nicosia.

The long-term restoration of the Polirone Monastic Complex of San Benedetto Po stabilised earthquake damage, conserved historic fabric, and reactivated the site with new cultural and civic functions.

Find out more about the Polirone Monastic Complex

The Cypriot Fiddler research project documents the life stories of traditional Greek Cypriot and Turkish Cypriot musicians. Through long-term fieldwork, a documentary, a book and an open-access website, it preserves oral storytelling, local dialects and a shared musical heritage.

Find out more about The Cypriot Fiddler

title: European Heritage Awards / Europa Nostra Awards 2026 ceremony
content:
The European Heritage Awards Ceremony 2026 was the highlight of the European Cultural Heritage Summit 2026 taking place from 26 to 30 May in Nicosia, Cyprus.

The event celebrated the 2026 winners, 30 outstanding heritage achievements from 18 European countries.

Five Grand Prix winners and the Public Choice Award winner were announced, each receiving a monetary award of €10 000.

Celebrating the winners of the 2026 European Heritage Awards.

Europa Nostra's dedicated website to the European Heritage Awards.

Celebrating the winners of the 2026 European Heritage Awards.
snippet: The European Heritage Awards Ceremony 2026 was the highlight of the European Cultural Heritage Summit 2026 taking place from 26 to 30 May in Nicosia, Cyprus.

title: EUmies Awards Days 2026
content:
Following the announcement of the winners in Oulu on 16 April, the EUmies Awards Days take place in the Mies van der Rohe Pavilion and Palau Victòria Eugènia in Barcelona on 11-12 May.

The EUmies Awards Days include the 2026 EUmies Awards Ceremony, the exhibition of the 2026 prize edition, and the EUmies Awards Talks.

The EUmies Awards Talks focus on the theme of reuse through a series of conversations, panels and encounters, the invited participants will share their experiences, processes and positions, addressing how architecture engages with existing conditions, resources and social contexts.

The Awards Ceremony takes place in the Mies van der Rohe Pavilion and will count with the participation of Normunds Popens, Deputy Director-General of the Directorate-General for Education, Youth, Sport and Culture at the European Commission. The EUmies Awards are supported by the Creative Europe programme of the European Union.

The EUmies Awards Talks count with the participation of authors of the winning and finalist works as well as their clients. Fellows of the LINA European architecture platform will also present their work.
snippet: Following the announcement of the winners in Oulu on 16 April, the EUmies Awards Days take place in the Mies van der Rohe Pavilion and Palau Victòria Eugènia in Barcelona on 11-12 May.

title: Reading and literacy – a joint approach across education and culture
content:
The Literacy for Democracy conference gathered over 200 participants in Warsaw. It emphasised the human dimension of reading, highlighting that literacy skills are central to addressing Europe’s current challenges, including competitiveness and democracy.

The conference brought together policymakers, reading promotion specialists, paediatricians, book sector and business community representatives. They shared experiences and discussed key issues, such as how to support parents and caregivers in their role as influencers of young minds, and the policy measures already in place as well as potential areas for enhancement.

A two half-day workshop followed the conference to prepare the foundations of the EU Literacy Coalition, that will be established in autumn 2026 as part of the Union of Skills/ the Action Plan on Basic Skills initiative.

The joint events illustrate the European Commission’s approach to bringing together the cultural, creative and book sectors with the education community and beyond to tackle the decline in literacy competences and reading habits.

title: Simone Veil Prize: first call for entries published
content:
The Simone Veil Prize published its first call for entries on 18 May 2026. The prize highlights how cultural heritage and places of memory can enable civic participation, education and democratic engagement, reflecting the important contribution of Jewish cultural heritage to Europe’s shared history.

The application period is open until 31 July 2026.

Winners will be presented with awards in a ceremony in Brussels at the end of this year.

The prize promotes heritage as a positive, forward-looking force for respectful coexistence in European society. It reflects the European Union’s commitment to safeguarding cultural diversity, countering discrimination and antisemitism, and promoting shared historical perspectives.

It honours Simone Veil, French Holocaust survivor, who dedicated her life to reconciliation, passionately promoting human rights and European unity. As the first president of the European Parliament, she campaigned against antisemitism and fought for a unified Europe of shared ideals, making her a powerful symbol of the values the prize seeks to celebrate.

In her speech at the Euro-Chanukah 2025 celebration, President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen said:

title: Simone Veil Prize
content:
The Simone Veil Prize is an annual European cultural prize funded under the Creative Europe programme promoting heritage as a positive, forward-looking force for respectful coexistence in European society. It reflects the European Union’s commitment to safeguarding cultural diversity, countering discrimination and antisemitism, and promoting shared historical perspectives.

The prize is rooted in the values of the Creative Europe programme and aligned with the EU strategy on combating antisemitism and fostering Jewish life (2021-2030).

In her speech at the Euro-Chanukah 2025 celebration, President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen said:

title: European Heritage Label: 13 new sites celebrated in Brussels
content:
Marking the culmination of the 2025 selection process, the European Heritage Label (EHL) ceremony took place at the Art and History Museum in Brussels. The heritage community gathered to recognise the 13 newly awarded sites.

The event brought together EHL national coordinators and sites, representatives from European institutions, cultural heritage experts, stakeholders and members of the European Parliament. The ceremony featured performances by participating sites, highlighting the diversity of Europe’s heritage and its role in promoting shared values.

European Commissioner for Intergenerational Fairness, Youth, Culture and Sport, Glenn Micallef opened the ceremony. 
Bogdan Andrzej Zdrojewski, Vice Chair of the Committee on Culture and Education of the European Parliament, 
Heiki Raab, Chair of the Commission for Social Policy, Education, Employment, Research and Culture of the Committee of the Regions, and 
Elena Theodoulou Charalambous, Chair of the Cultural Affairs Committee of the Council of Europe attended the event.

The 13 awarded sites reflect a wide diversity of heritage types, including

  • cultural institutions
  • industrial heritage
  • archaeological sites
  • places of memory
